Company Description:
Accurent, controlled by Davide Nappo delli Paoli, our Founding Managing Partner & Global Head for North America & EMEA, is a global investment banking organization, real estate, financial services and venture capital conglomerate headquartered in New York City and London.

Our firm has a long track record of publicly listing and funding companies via our $3.5billion fund partnership; advising leading private equity and venture-backed businesses, micro to large-cap public companies; as well as co-investing alongside family offices and UHNWIs. The firm’s specialties span capital raising or direct injection in the form of private equity and debt via our $3.5BLN fund partnership and venture capital arm; due diligence and deal sourcing, including preparation of business plans, investor materials and financial modeling; all the way to IPOs and RTOs, mergers & acquisitions, leveraged buyouts and divestitures, spinoffs, hostile takeovers and takeover defense, recapitalizations, strategic advisory with alternative asset management and everything in between.

We have built a robust network of strategic alliances, including top-tier angels, venture capital and private equity investors, UHNWIs, family offices, pension funds, government entities, fortune 500 companies, financial services conglomerates, real estate developers and alternative asset managers, to name a few, and a network of M&A-active operating companies to match. We also have a long track record of securities-related transactions and credentials at our broker/dealer, which spans virtually all industry verticals in the $5M-5BLN+ check size and valuation range.

Accurent Real Estate & Family Office, a world-class advisory and investment management group, covers private investments, commercial, hospitality and residential core to development real estate assets across the US and EMEA.

On the VC side, we construct our investment portfolio strategically, selecting companies to which we can drive long-term value.

Company Description:
Firm Overview:

Alpha Strategies has a team of highly experienced investment consultants and research analysts, each with a comprehensive understanding of both traditional and alternative investments. Our dynamic group of investment professionals has been sourcing unique opportunities in order to satisfy the growing demand for alternative investments since 2009. As the market landscape continues to evolve, investors are becoming more sophisticated, and demanding access to alternative investment strategies, which in the past were only available to institutions and the wealthiest individuals. Our firm works directly with family offices, institutional investors, consultants, investment advisors and high-net-worth Individuals, to help supplement their internal research efforts and identify best-in-class alpha generating managers early in their lifecycle. 

Capital Markets Group:

The Alpha Capital Markets Group is dedicated to helping early-stage and established fund managers increase assets under management by enhancing their sophistication, exposure and marketability. Our team works to supplement the fund manager’s current fundraising efforts by identifying qualified investor groups across the capital markets and wholesale distribution channels. We only work with managers who understand that growing assets under management requires a dedicated professional effort and cohesive cross-team synergies. Each member of our Capital Markets Group has an in-depth understanding of today’s complex investment environment, and is committed to delivering unbiased sales and marketing support to each manager on our platform.

Title: Partner
Company Product and Services: investment banking, mergers & acquisitions, sell your business, exit advantage program
Company Description:
You’ve spent a lifetime creating a thriving business through careful attention, hard work, and dedication; a business that has fulfilled your dreams, supported your family, and contributed to the livelihood of the employees. When you are finally ready to move on to the next phase of your life, you want to sell that business with that same sense of pride, in a manner that maximizes value to both you and the buyer. You need a partner that understands and feels the same way about their business. You need Touchstone Advisors.

Touchstone Advisors have been representing business owners for over 20 years, helping them prepare and position their company for sale. Focusing primarily on privately held and family businesses with revenues between $5 - $50 million in manufacturing, distribution, technology, and business-to-business services, 

Touchstone Advisors applies the same investment banking merger and acquisition model used on Wall Street. Title: Managing Partner, President, BOD
Company Description:
In 1967, Forstmann Leff & Associates was founded as private investment partnership with the very specific objective of managing other people’s money intensively for high rates of return. Risk and reward were the constant determinants of capital commitments. Long, short, and cash were the order of the day based on internal and external research. The firm was one of the first hedge funds in the US, following the AW Jones model that had proven to be a novel way to invest partnership money.

The firm was a leader in attracting major corporate accounts, who were searching for more aggressive and innovative ways to deploy their pension funds. Clients included, but not limited to: Intel, Pfizer, Standard Oil of Indiana, The Pension Benefit Guarantee Corp, The Ford Family, Northrop, Honeywell, Lockheed, Northwest Airlines, Knight Ridder, Delta, MCA, Raychem, Chrysler, City of New York, State of Minnesota, State of Maine, Holy Cross University, The Archdiocese of New York, The Major League Players Pension.

In 1975, Mr. Theodore Forstmann, a brother, lawyer, and new business partner, at Forstmann Leff & Associates, decided to form a partnership with Mr. Nick Forstmann, who was at JP Morgan, and Mr. Brian Little, a banker at White Weld, to build the second Leverage Buy-Out firm in the US. With the financial support of Forstmann Leff & Associates and the introduction to their clients, Forstmann Little was off to a fast start.

At its peak in the late 1990s, Forstmann Little was among the largest LBO/private equity firms. Since its inception, the firm had made more than 30 significant acquisitions, including: IMG, ABC Citadel, Gulfstream Aerospace, Dr. Pepper, 24 Hour Fitness, Community Health Hospitals, General Instruments.

In 1998, Forstmann & Co. was formed as a merchant bank and a private investment firm focused on acquiring, advising, investing and scaling businesses with strong management teams that can create and execute magnitude of change to its respective industry.

Every transaction involves the absolute assignment of whole commercial receivables under UCC Article 9. Not loans. Not securities. We hold a direct ownership position in the receivable.

Business Model & Structure

Wyoming Entity
All engagements are governed strictly by the laws of the State of Wyoming.

Service-Only Compensation
UBC charges fixed administrative service fees only. We do not take profit participation, carried interest, or asset management fees.

True Sale
Transactions involve the absolute assignment of whole commercial receivables (UCC Article 9 payment intangibles), not loans or securities.

Eligibility & Restrictions
Services are restricted strictly to institutional participants and commercial entities capable of transacting in aggregate tranches of $1,000,000 or greater. UBC operates exclusively in authorized jurisdictions and does not solicit, source assets from, purchase assets from, or facilitate transactions with entities located or organized in jurisdictions where such activities would require specific licensing or regulatory approval that UBC does not currently maintain.

Geographic availability is subject to change, and all transactions are void where prohibited by local law.

Legal Disclaimer
UBC operates as a technical service provider and is not a broker-dealer, investment adviser, asset manager, or chartered bank. UBC does not provide investment advice, valuation opinions, or discretionary management of funds. UBC does not operate pooled investment vehicles or engage in general solicitation.

Title: Managing Director, Consulting
Company Product and Services: corporate development, healthcare services, mergers and acquisitions, mental health clinic ops
Company Description:
Selling or growing a healthcare company—especially in behavioral and mental health—is one of the most important and complex decisions a founder or investor will face. The process is high-stakes, emotional, and often confusing: valuations, compliance, reimbursement, buyer fit, due diligence, deal structure, and timing all matter. Navigating it alone leads to uncertainty, missed opportunities, and undervalued outcomes.

That’s where we come in.

Healthcare Capital Advisors is a specialized investment bank built exclusively for lower middle-market behavioral health providers. We combine deep industry knowledge with hands-on M&A expertise to guide owners and investors through every stage of a transaction—confidently and strategically.

Who We Help
• Clinic owners preparing for a sale (70% of clients)
• Strategic and financial buyers seeking quality acquisitions (30%)

Whether you’re selling, acquiring, or positioning your clinic for the future, we help you avoid costly missteps, understand your true value, and make decisions rooted in clarity—not guesswork.

What We Offer
• Sell-side & buy-side M&A advisory
• Operational and strategic consulting to strengthen value drivers
• M&A Bootcamp, a step-by-step program to prepare your business for a successful transaction

Our mission is simple: help behavioral health leaders build stronger businesses and achieve outcomes that honor both their financial goals and their commitment to patient care.

We don’t just execute transactions—we guide people through pivotal moments. With Healthcare Capital Advisors, you gain a partner who understands your industry, protects your vision, and stays by your side every step of the way.

Company Description:
ShoreHaven Wealth Partners is an independent wealth management firm working with a select group of affluent multigenerational families and high net worth individuals, to protect, grow and transition their assets. Many of our clients are family-owned businesses who face succession and transition challenges.  

ShoreHaven walks you through a step-by-step journey with clear communications and transparent actions, from identifying priorities and goals, to recommending and implementing solutions, to monitoring and rebalancing portfolios.

We are especially experienced in assessing the impact of family dynamics on the strategies we recommend. Likewise, we are experienced in helping clients work through one-off events that impact their financial situation, from the death of a loved one to the settlement of a divorce to the sale of a business.  

With your input and approval, we will also construct and execute a discretionary investment strategy, using ShoreHaven’s own set of personalized portfolios. Very importantly, we will carefully monitor ongoing performance of your investments, making prudent modifications as needed, to react to market conditions and changing circumstances in your life.  

ShoreHaven will collaborate with your other trusted advisors, such as attorneys and accountants, to ensure your customized financial plan is developed holistically and executed accurately and efficiently.

Title: Partner
Company Description:
Every important decision deserves clear guidance.

QS Capital Advisors works with owners of high value companies who want to understand their options and move through the sale process with clarity. Selling a company is serious work. Early offers anchor expectations. Buyers shape the story in ways that suit their plans. Without a steady process, the final outcome can drift from what the owner intended.

We advise owners through complex sales so they can secure a strong valuation, choose the right partner, and move forward with confidence. Our team presents your business to buyers in a clear and compelling way, manages the information flow, and creates the organized competition that reveals the true value of the company.

Our experience includes more than $700M in closed transactions across multiple industries. We have worked with owners who want a clear view of their position and a process that protects what they have built.

Our approach is simple.

Meet Up
We begin with a confidential conversation to understand your goals and concerns.

Team Up
We review your position, prepare your story, and show how the market will see your company.

Rest Easy
We manage the sale from start to finish and guide you through each step so you can make decisions with confidence.

You built something important. When you are considering a sale, you deserve accurate information, careful guidance, and a steady process. If you are thinking about your future or beginning to explore your options, we are here to help you understand the road ahead.

Company Description:
Allegiance Capital is an M&A investment bank that has been providing expert financial services to mid-sized, privately held companies since 1998. Our specialty is representing privately owned companies with between $20 million and $500 million in annual revenue. 

Our clients typically are business owners who want to sell the company, transition to new management, or bring on a financial partner to grow their business. Our goal is to be their trusted advisor.  

Allegiance Capital began as a solution to a problem that founder David Mahmood had experienced himself and wanted to help other entrepreneurs avoid.

During his business career, which spans more than 50 years, Mr. Mahmood merged and sold seven companies that he built from scratch. To do that, he hired a number of investment banks and M&A attorneys. Most were bright people who understood the process, but they didn’t understand how to market his companies.

Company Description:
Cowen Prime Services was formed in 2015 following Cowen Group’s (Nasdaq: COWN)  acquisition of Concept Capital Markets, LLC. 

Cowen Prime Services LLC offers a comprehensive suite of brokerage and related services that provide traditional and alternative investment managers with solutions that are customizable and scalable. The firm was built by former investment managers to serve hedge fund managers, managed account platforms, institutional investors, family offices, and registered investment advisers with turn-key solutions designed to free its clients to focus on their core competencies. Our offering features world-class custody and clearing options, multi asset class capabilities, leading execution and order management systems, a seasoned execution desk, a range of financing options, a highly professional operations and customer support team, comprehensive portfolio reporting capabilities, and capital introduction.

Cowen Prime Services LLC is headquartered in Garden City, New York and operates offices in New York City, Greenwich, CT, Alpharetta, GA, Chicago, IL, Santa Monica, CA and El Segundo, CA. The firm is registered as a Broker Dealer and Investment Adviser with the SEC, and is a member of FINRA, NFA, and SIPC.

Our parent company is Cowen Group, a publicly-traded diversified financial services firm with a rich history that reflects the focus and entrepreneurial spirit that continues to characterize the firm.  Since its formation in 1918 as a small bond brokerage business in the heart of New York’s financial district, Cowen Group has grown into a leading diversified financial services company with operating businesses focused on the value of active management and alpha generation.

Company Description:
At DAK, we work hand-in-hand with CEOs and owners of middle-market and family businesses who are interested in growing their company through acquisitions or selling all or part of their company now or in the future. We assist in planning for and executing transactions and maximizing value for owners and shareholders.  In addition, we assist in financial restructurings, capital raises and prepare valuations and fairness opinions.

Our clients work directly with senior-level M&A professionals who have in-depth market knowledge, access to a global network of targets, savvy negotiation skills, and a strong strategic focus in addressing such topics as: 
•	Determining  the right time to buy or sell a business 
•	Where to begin & key steps  to prepare for an acquisition or sale 
•	Obstacles companies face in a sale or acquisition
•	Critical factors  to maximize a company's value
•	Finding the right buyer for the business
•	Factors driving a successful acquisition or divestiture 

Since our founding in 1984, DAK's goal has been to maximize the value of middle market companies and optimize the transaction experience: 600+ transactions later, we have done just that! Our knowledge spans dozens of industries including: manufacturing, distribution, technology, consumer products, business services, food, packaging, telecommunications, healthcare and more. 

Our website (https://www.dakgroup.com) provides lots of useful information for business owners, including how to begin thinking about and preparing for a transaction.  Call anytime for a complimentary consultation: Joan McGeough 201-478-5261.

Company Description:
We transition businesses to management teams, key employees, strategic acquirers, and family members in an effective, confidential, and professional way.

Sometimes, this takes the form of an internal buyout to a family member or a key employee. Other times, the best path forward is with a strategically-aligned partner or competitor that knows the company well. Company Description:
AN ENTREPRENEUR'S INVESTMENT BANK

SEA provides investment banking services to entrepreneurial companies with EBITDA from $2 million to $8 million. We help owners achieve their ultimate exit by focusing our process on strategic buyers willing to pay higher, strategic multiples. We understand what you’ve been through building your business and make certain you get paid for it. We focus on:

     •     M&A - SEA employs a three-step investment banking process that helps 
           entrepreneur's exit successfully. That means working closely with you, the 
           entrepreneur, and your team (accountants, lawyers, wealth managers etc.) 
           throughout the process. This is a highly collaborative process wrought 
           with the emotion of your business. We have evolved and grown since we 
           started in 2006, but our focus on the entrepreneur and commitment to 
           exceeding client’s expectations has never changed. 
 
     •    Outsourced M&A - Quality companies don’t go unnoticed for long. If 
           you’ve found SEA, there’s a good chance you’ve built a great company. It 
           also means that competitors, strategic partners, or financial buyers may 
           have already shown interest in acquiring your firm. As your advisor, we 
           will tell you what to expect and guide you through the process. We’ll 
           negotiate on your behalf for the deal structure you want and deserve. 
           We’ll take in all the due diligence requests and help to answer them. We 
           will do whatever it takes to get the deal across the finish line.

We are a team of former operators, entrepreneurs, and bulge-bracket bankers. There’s no charge to talk, and no question is a dumb one. We look forward to hearing your story.

Our specialty is introducing those companies to ""Mezzanine"" funds. These are funds with longer term vision, but have the flexibility and vision to invest in small, earlier stage companies. Company Description:
Onasander ICO is the first Decentralized Investment Fund.

Executive Summary

This is LinkedIn Business Page for building the first decentralized investment bank crowdfunded using blockchain technology.  The plan to open up an investment bank is to build an investment fund first.  The fund, including our ICO tokens will be backed by tradable assets of this world.  Simply put, by entire world economy.  With time and trading the fund will be big enough to be converted into an investment bank.

Highlights
The investment fund will pay 10% dividend to all of our Onasander ONA token holders, as percentage of profits will be quarterly distributed to those who invested in our company.  Onasander fund will turn regular profits, because of our experience, and because of its unique Asset Price Analysis Software called Onasander.  Below is a chart from our Onasander trading application presenting most overpriced assets as of March 2018.

Objectives
Our goal is to execute a very successful ICO, open up an investment fund, and take advantage of the upcoming stock recession, volatility, and price swings, all along benefitting our investors with dividends, and high token valuations.  With time, the objective will be to manage wealth for our clients and grow the bank.

Mission Statement
The climate for opening up an investment fund is perfect.  We are living in interesting times.  Free money flowing from central banks to in debt and bankrupt governments around the world created a major global bubble in many asset classes, where prices have reached an extreme.  We are standing in front of an opportunity to open up a financial institution and make tremendous financial gains right from the start.  The upcoming recession in the world financial markets will create price volatility, and opportunities for years to come.  Using our unique Onasander trading software, we can foresee price movements and benefit from it.  

Keys to Success
The Asset Price Analysis Software is the key to our success.  Our market tools developed over two decades have no competition in the financial world.  They allow us to predict market moves, see upcoming recessions, identify bubbles, find major tops, spot asset price bottoms, and successfully trade in all market conditions.

Title: Principal
Company Product and Services: real estate, investment banking
Company Description:
Madderra & Cazalot is a commercial mortgage banking firm with offices in New Orleans, Louisiana and Dallas, Texas. The company was formed in early 2002 through a partnership of New Orleans-based J.M. Madderra & Company, Inc., a commercial mortgage banking firm founded in 1993, and Dallas-based Ron Cazalot.

Madderra & Cazalot formally represents Prudential Mortgage Capital, Key Bank Real Estate Capital Markets and Reilly Mortgage as a correspondent on all permanent loan products offered by these lenders. Madderra & Cazalot is a very dominant player in the financing of multifamily properties in Louisiana due largely to the excellent track record compiled by Mr. Madderra and his firm. Since its inception, J.M. Madderra and now Madderra & Cazalot has closed permanent loans and equity transactions on Gulf Coast projects totaling nearly one billion dollars.

In addition, Madderra & Cazalot is widely recognized in the New Orleans area as the foremost authority on the New Orleans multifamily market. Since early 2001, the company has produced “The Greater New Orleans Multi-Family Report” which tracks all major projects in the area and compiles rent, occupancy, sales and trends for each sub-market and the overall multi-family market. 

With the opening of the Dallas office in 2002, Madderra & Cazalot has now taken on a larger geographic focus with Texas and the surrounding states becoming central to the firm’s mission. Mr. Cazalot has resided in Texas since 1979 and has been responsible for financing permanent loans totaling over $500 million over the past five years.

Company Description:
31 Years in the making. A boutique full-service investment bank.

Leveraging Univest’s unique geographic roots in Asia Pacific and North America, and our hybrid position across institutions and retail, we provide a variety of services to small-to-medium size public and private enterprises through our Investment Banking and Corporate Financing Division including mergers & acquisitions capital markets advisory, valuation and fairness of opinion advisory, private placement, initial and secondary public offerings.

Unconventional to our many transactional focused peers, our team takes a fundamental value-driven approach. As generalists, we are focused on discovering innovative, exciting, and emerging growth companies to provide them with long-term strategic advisory service and unique global access to capital. Our seasoned banking team is adept in heavy lifting situations and pride themselves on staying ahead of curve. We believe in mutual value generation for enterprises and investors driven by capital, innovation, and sound market execution.

Trust and strategic partnerships are the foundation of our equity and debt capital markets strategy. Leveraging our strong, personal relationships with the most long-term institutional buy-side investors, we structure and execute customized financing solutions. Our capital markets team introduces the right partners to address a broad range of capital needs, including buyouts, growth capital, platform formation, and project financing for corporate issuers and financial sponsors. Our family-oriented team combined with our global reach and vast network of institutional partners, protects the interests of and mutually benefits both our issuers and our investors, leading to efficient execution, improved valuation, and successful transactions for our clients. With deep-rooted relationships, we continue to build valuable connections with new clients to optimize their capital structure.

Title: Managing Director
Company Product and Services: investment banking, mergers & acquisitions, corporate finance, sell-side advisory, buy-side advisory, capital sourcing, recapitalizations, restructuring, capital raise, growth capital, dividend recapitalization, owner transition
Company Description:
We are a lower middle market Investment Banking firm providing privately held businesses with sell-side representation, merger and acquisition advisory and debt and equity capital raising representation. As past business owners, private equity investors and operators, we optimize our clients’ positioning in market and find the right buyer/partner/capital provider for them. We have the experience and knowledge of the market to bring their company to market and manage the process to their "telos"​ - their ultimate objective. 

Our sell-side clients are typically founders or owners whose companies have EBITDA (cash flow) of $2 million or more per year. They want to enter a new phase of their life even if they want to remain a partial owner and active in the business. They seek to diversify their assets, reduce their risk in the business, and reduce their involvement and responsibilities. 

Our merger and acquisition advisory clients do not need full representation. Possibly they have received an offer to sell or they want to make an acquisition. In either case they recognize the value of having a third party financial advisor with the experience and market knowledge to help guide them to a successful outcome.

Raising debt or equity capital requires constant involvement in the ever-changing marketplace and strong relationships with providers of all stripes. We serve established companies with a history of revenue and profit, even if they are currently experiencing challenges. We do not raise capital for start-ups or early stage companies. Our clients use the capital to fund growth, acquisitions, dividend recapitalizations to diversify their assets and risk, buying out shareholders or partners, replace an existing creditor with a desire to leave, and many other reasons.

Member FINRA/SIPC

Title: Managing Director
Company Description:
Farm Creek Advisors is a system-level intelligence firm focused on how energy, capital, and industrial markets actually work.

Most firms analyze markets through price, headlines, or individual transactions.

We focus on the layer beneath that:

how systems clear.

In today’s environment, markets are not defined by supply alone. They are defined by movement—by logistics, capital flow, infrastructure constraints, and the ability of systems to function under stress.

That shift changes everything.

In energy, the constraint is no longer just production. It is transport, insurance, routing, and refining compatibility.

In capital markets, the constraint is not capital formation. It is liquidity, exit pathways, and the mobility of capital through the system.

Across industrial and digital infrastructure, the constraint is not technology alone. It is governance, integration, and operational resilience.

These are not separate problems.

They are expressions of the same underlying dynamic:

systems under pressure behave differently than systems in equilibrium.

Farm Creek operates at that intersection.

Our work spans:

• Energy & Logistics — crude, refined products, shipping, and infrastructure dynamics  
• Capital & Valuation — private capital, M&A, liquidity, and exit pathways  
• Industrial & Cyber Systems — operational technology, resilience, and governance  

We advise operators, investors, and decision-makers who need to understand not just what is happening—but why the system is behaving the way it is.

Because in complex markets, price is the output.

System structure is the cause.

---

Farm Creek Advisors  
System-Level Intelligence for Energy, Capital, and Industrial Marke

Beachside Capital, LLC (Beachside) is a private equity investment firm focused on growth markets. Beachside’s mission is to launch, grow and turn-around businesses. We invest in mature markets where new concepts are first tested and old ones can be rejuvenated---as well as selected emerging markets, where advanced products, services and business practices become catalysts for growth.

	Beachside invests in companies and products where by applying proven management and financial techniques, “breakthrough” opportunities for growth and change can result. When evaluating investment opportunities, Beachside professionals look for situations where future growth potential is high, effective management can enhance performance significantly and current executives and owners welcome the involvement of an active investor group. We back strong management teams who can benefit from the knowledge, experience and contacts that Beachside’s principals possess. We are committed to help promising consumer product and service companies achieve their full potential.

Title: Managing Partner
Company Industry: venture capital and private equity principals
Company Product and Services: private equity, m&a, investment banking
Company Description:
Enter Capital is a Private Investment Office and M&A advisory firm serving private equity sponsors.

Enter Capital was started by Andrew Ponomar and Elena Nosova, who moved to the U.S. in 2013 from Eastern Europe and Cyprus and chose to build long-term in the U.S. The firm initially started as a two-sided marketplace for sourcing M&A opportunities; the software was discontinued as a standalone business, but the tech skills and respect for engineering teams gained from building it remain core to who the team is.

Enter Capital then transitioned into an M&A advisory business and now focuses on investing in SaaS and IT services companies as a Private Investment Office and buy-side M&A advisor to multibillion-dollar private equity firms on both coasts, and PE-backed platform companies.

Alex Kudriashov and Chris Wang are advisors and partners, involved on a case-by-case basis in deals, funds, strategic and legal support.

Title: Managing Director
Company Description:
As an independent financial advisor, we work closely with our clients to tailor optimal strategic solutions that meet their objectives, unburdened by the internal and external pressures felt by larger investment banks. 

Legado Capital Advisors was formed with one goal in mind - be different, be better, leave a legacy. 

The team at Legado Capital Advisors offers a full suite of investment banking services designed to navigate the evolving energy capital markets. 

Our strong relationships with capital providers including insurance companies, large family offices, and institutional investors, provides Legado a unique capability to source capital solutions for both debt and equity needs.

Our highly talented team thrives on creative solutions and remains dedicated to achieving the high standards our clients expect.

Title: Managing Partner
Company Product and Services: investment banking (including m&a advisory and capital raising), asset management, merchant banking
Company Description:
XMS Capital Partners, LLC (“XMS”) was launched in August, 2006 with the goal of creating a different kind of investment banking boutique, a place where relationships are paramount and more important than transactional velocity and where advice is rendered to clients over a long period of time and across their critical strategic and financial issues. 

Since 2006, XMS has grown into a global, independent financial services firm providing investment banking, asset management and merchant banking to clients. It now has offices in Chicago, London and Dublin and strategic relationships in Germany, Japan, China and India. It also has a strategic alliance with Headwall Partners for its expertise in the steel, metals, mining and building products industries.

XMS Capital Partners has three business units: Investment Banking (including M&A Advisory and Capital Raising), Asset Management and Merchant Banking.

Investment Banking 

XMS works with public and private companies, including family-owned businesses. XMS has completed a wide range of complex domestic and international transactions across multiple industries: broad industrials, business services, automotive, paper and packaging, chemicals, consumer/food/beverage, healthcare, financial services, energy, agriculture, aerospace/defense, distribution/logistics/transport, education, retail/restaurants, tech/media/telecom and sports.

Asset Management 

XA Investments LLC (”XAI”) is a Chicago-based firm founded by XMS in March, 2016. The XAI leadership team believes that financial advisors and their clients need access to a broader range of alternative investment strategies and managers. XAI is the registered funds division of XMS.

Merchant Banking

XMS merchant banking activities are focused on partnering with strong management teams and creating tailored investment solutions. XMS differs from traditional private equity firms, since XMS is not bound by specific hold periods or a requirement for control.

Title: Director
Company Description:
For nearly two decades, Cedel group has attained a significant number of businesses that were initially related to finance, the stock market and the banking world; nonetheless, Cedel has diversified into multiple business areas.

Currently, Cedel’s portfolio is comprised of active investments in a broad array of domestic and international sectors. Cedel owns a controlling share of the VMe Network - the 4th largest Spanish Television Network in the United States currently broadcast to more than 70 million U.S. households via cable, satellite, and over-the-air distribution. In addition to VME TV, Cedel owns a controlling share in VMe Kids and a minority share in Nuevo Mundo TV, a Spanish Television Network based in Montreal, Canada. Cedel also has investments in manufacturing, aviation, and real estate.

Title: Partner
Company Product and Services: sell-side advisory, buy-side advisory, mergers & acquisitions, capital raising, strategic advisory, restructuring & special situations
Company Description:
Cobaltum Partners is an independent merchant banking firm dedicated to providing independent advice, high-quality execution and unparalleled access to value-added sources of capital to our clients and partners.  We specialize in advising entrepreneur- and family-owned enterprises, regional firms and multinational corporations based in the U.S. and Latin America on mergers & acquisitions and private capital transactions.

We strive to be trusted advisors by placing our clients’ interests above all else.  We are committed to long-term client relationships, seeking to add value at each stage of our clients’ development, from raising capital and advising on growth opportunities, to evaluating strategic alternatives and realizing liquidity events.  Our proximity to our clients enables us to understand their needs, set objectives, evaluate alternatives, craft optimal solutions and execute successful transactions designed to maximize value and deliver results for stakeholders.

We believe that the key to our long-term success is working for and with great clients – the kind we would invest our own capital in.  This guiding belief is the essence behind our Principal Investing activities. Cobaltum stands ready to invest in, alongside, or in partnership with our clients as a value-added partner.

Cobaltum’s principals have been involved in more than 50 middle market transactions, including mergers and acquisitions, divestitures, equity raises, debt issuances, recapitalizations, restructurings and direct investments.  Our clients benefit from our extensive network, including long-standing relationships with premier institutional sources of capital (private equity groups, hedge funds, venture capitalists, commercial banks, special situations investors and strategic buyers) across the Americas.

Company Description:
Founded in 2006, Credit Capital Investments (CCI), LLC is a global investment manager, advisor to Teak Hill Fund, Teak Hill Indus Fund, Income Priority Fund and recently launched, technology-driven Assay Plus Fund. CCI also manages SMA's for individual investors.

Teak Hill Fund applies a rigorous, multi-layered approach combining thematic and deeply researched insights to its overall investment selection process. The fund further levers our group's advanced technological capabilities and quantitative analytics to identify prospective opportunities for investment across the full spectrum of the capital structure.
 
CCI's Income Priority Fund focuses on the leveraged finance market utilizing a deep, relative value approach to generate superior risk-adjusted returns. The strategy tactically blends a meticulously researched combination of floating-rate syndicated loans and high-yield securities seeking improved risk-adjusted returns versus comparable income producing portfolios.

While our core investing principles remain constant, we have a history of perpetual innovation, interweaving our human capital with cutting-edge technology. The name “Teak Hill” evokes the idea of both timeless strength and resilience.

CCI's senior team is comprised of notably experienced asset management professionals, who have excelled in previous leadership roles at highly-respected institutions. They are complemented by experienced managers, analysts, and staff. Together they form a close-knit team and are correspondingly invest in our fund offerings alongside our limited partners and shareholders.

CCI is a registered investment advisor with the United States SEC.

Title: Co-Founder & Chief Information Officer
Company Product and Services: m&a network, m&a marketplace, middle market
Company Description:
M&A Nexus makes it easy for CEOs and CFOs to connect with investment banker for their mandates. Currently, most M&A marketplaces are dedicated to actually selling the business rather than running a bake off to determine which investment banker to actually use. This causes many businesses to run an inefficient process leading to an emotionally driven advisor decision. M&A Nexus creates a place where CEOs and CFOs can run their bake offs in an efficient manner.

It's unique because the platform is entirely dedicated to helping CEOs and CFOs decide which investment banker to use. It's special because businesses share their Mandate allowing investment bankers to learn more about their buy or sell side needs and then to connect.

The big benefit is that it helps CEOs and CFOs run an effective bake off in order to discover the right investment banker to work with. This helps them avoid search costs traditionally related to the procurement of an advisor.

Feature #1: Investment banker network
Benefit: Makes it simple to connect with investment bankers which eliminates the search costs of finding an advisor

Feature #2: Bake off marketplace
Benefit: Makes it simple for investment bankers to learn about buy and sell side mandates from businesses within their coverage area. Once an investment banker finds a Mandate that overlaps with their coverage, they can then connect with that business and participate in the bake off.

M&A Nexus makes it easy for CEOs and CFOs to connect with investment bankers. Businesses can create a profile and immediately start connecting with potential advisors by running a digital bake off. Fill out your Mandate now and start connecting at www.MnANexus.com

Title: Managing Partner
Company Product and Services: investment banking, venture capital, oncology, drug development, diagnostics
Company Description:
In today’s challenging market, development stage oncology companies are faced with increasing pressure from all sides. Institutional investors are continuing to defer investment until companies are well into clinical development. Likewise, pharmaceutical companies are demanding greater data to “derisk” assets prior to entering into a license or co-development deal.

This environment is placing enormous strain on development stage companies. Most have limited resources with which to generate the compelling data set required to attract larger sources of capital and are thus stuck, sometimes for years, in a quagmire of constraints imposed by the limits of financing rather than developing the potential of the technology and opportunity they envision.

Our team has the ability to identify companies with promising technologies and vision that are caught in this gap that exists in the capital formation process between early stage investors and venture/strategic investors. We have the expertise to determine the precise steps which will robustly demonstrate the potential of the technology and will develop a comprehensive story that will attract institutional investment. We have the resources to assist our clients in addressing any deficiencies that may be present including gaps in the management team, recruitment of key opinion leaders, etc.  Finally we have the network to bridge the “gap” financing chasm, thereby opening the company up to a variety of larger funding opportunities.

Company Description:
Gordian Group is a leading national investment bank specializing in financial restructurings,  complex and/or "story"​ financings and distressed/stressed/complex M&A assignments. We are a boutique M&A¹ and strategic finance advisory firm based in Northeast Ohio with tethers throughout the USA.

Our seasoned business and finance professionals have experience in manufacturing, investment banking, and direct investing from organizations such as The Coca-Cola Company, Kent Watersports (private equity owned), Huron Capital Partners, Goldman Sachs, SG Cowen Securities, Société Générale, Wedbush Morgan, Texas Industries, Chaparral Steel, P&G, and others.

Services Snapshot

• M&A: Transactions related to the sale or purchase of eligible private companies generating up to $25m EBITDA and/or $250m revenues annually.²

• Corporate finance: flexible debt/equity for profitable growth, improved capital structure, refinancing (better terms and duration), recapitalization (owners may take some wealth off the table), and non-owner management buyouts.

• Pre- and post-transaction support and advisory: monitoring/improving EBITDA and key performance indicators (KPIs); advising Boards and ownership groups as a member/non-member; ensuring compliance with financial covenants; expanding business and professional networks.

• Fees: negotiable per project/transaction such as (1) flat dollar amount, (2) hybrid (flat + variable), (3) 100% variable (no upfront fees; entirely performance-based), and (4) mostly non-cash, deferred/co-investment in a deal/transaction (referred to as merchant banking).

• Experienced working with privately owned companies; private equity, venture capital, and hedge fund firms; holding companies and family investment offices; and, publicly traded companies.

• Buy-and-build, growth-oriented, turnaround/restructuring, and ownership-transition assignments are some of our favorites.

Company Product and Services: private equity, mezzanine debt, investment management, leveraged buyouts
Company Description:
Cloud Equity Group is a Wall St. based investment management firm strategically positioned to capitalize on the expanding industries of web hosting and cloud-based infrastructure. The firm's primary focus is leveraging investor capital (affluent individuals, family offices, and large corporate accounts) in the acquisition and consolidation of private lower middle market companies that possess high levels of unlevered free cash flow and/or significant opportunity for exponential growth. 

Current portfolio initiatives target a build-up strategy and have garnered significant investor interest as the firm’s efforts are specialized, focused, and transparent within an industry the partners have superior expertise. The company generates returns for investors through a combination of debt and equity offerings, with options for current income and/or capital appreciation over a 3-6 year time horizon. By focusing investments in established, high cash flow, profitable companies they provide investors diversification into the private technology sector while avoiding the unnecessary risk of venture and start-up capital. 

CEG strives for excellence in delivering superior non-correlated returns while bringing ease and transparency to private market investments. After nearly two dozen successful transactions, their proven track record speaks for itself. The firm invites you to contact them if you're interested in learning more (info@cloudequitygroup.com) (212 618-1298) .

Title: Managing Director
Company Description:
After 15 successful years, The Chicago Corporation (TCC) will be winding down operations at year end 2024. This decision marks the end of an incredible journey, during which we have proudly served our clients as trusted advisors with dedication and expertise.

Since our inception, TCC has focused on providing best-in-class advisory services. Our experienced team has been committed to empowering middle-market companies, particularly those owned by entrepreneurs, families, and Private Equity Firms, to achieve their strategic goals.

Key Highlights of Our 15-Year Legacy:
•	Expert Guidance: We have successfully advised a diverse range of clients through complex transactions, offering tailored solutions that meet their unique needs.
•	Impactful Transactions: Over the years, TCC has facilitated numerous mergers, acquisitions, and capital raises, contributing significantly to the growth and success of our clients.
•	Strong Relationships: Our focus on building lasting partnerships has allowed us to foster trust and collaboration, enabling us to serve our clients effectively.
•	Commitment to Excellence: Our team of seasoned professionals has consistently delivered innovative strategies and insights, helping clients navigate an evolving market landscape.

We extend our heartfelt gratitude to everyone who has been part of the TCC journey. Your trust, collaboration, and support have been the foundation of our success, and we cherish the relationships we’ve built over the years.
Thank you for allowing us to be a part of your success story. We look forward to staying connected and wish you all continued prosperity in the future.

Company Description:
Alliant Partners is an investment banking and advisory firm serving middle-market technology companies globally.  Founded in 2017, the firm is reviving the Alliant name--and the premise that clients deserve personalized, hands-on advice on M&A and capital raising transactions from deeply experienced professionals. 

Alliant's founders have more than 60 years'​ combined experience, solely focused on advising technology companies,  and they have closed more than 300 transactions, comprising mergers, acquisitions, private placements of equity and debt, public financings, divestures, spin-offs and corporate partnerships.  Their experience traverses key sectors of the technology industry, including software/SaaS, communications equipment, wireless infrastructure, mobile, semiconductors, test and measurement, Internet infrastructure, B2C, B2B, services and integrated systems.

Alliant was founded on,  and operates with, the belief that middle-market transactions often have a degree of complexity that demands the full-time attention of seasoned bankers who are capable of drawing upon deep reservoirs of experience as both bankers and senior operators,  trusted relationships, and a thorough understanding of how success is defined among disparate stakeholders. Our team applies this practiced expertise to deliver successful outcomes.  We are patiently relentless. 

The Alliant name is synonymous with client-centered technology investment banking.  Originally co-founded in 1992 by Jim Kochman,  Alliant grew to be one of the leading advisors to technology companies and was acquired by Silicon Valley Bank in 2001.  The original Alliant  ceased operations in 2005,  but Jim Kochman and Scott Roth, colleagues at Pagemill Partners, felt that the  Alliant name embodied the values and foundation upon which their new firm, "new"​ Alliant Partners, was created.  Together, they share a vision for new Alliant as a pre-eminent advisor to leading technology companies and their investors.

Title: Principal
Company Product and Services: flat fee only financial planning & investment management, retirement income planning, college education funding, integrated tax planning, business planning, estate & charitable gift planning, insurance review
Company Description:
Integrity Wealth Solutions’ vision is to provide clients with comprehensive wealth management in a fair and equitable manner. To us, the word “integrity” means always putting the client first, doing the right thing, and eliminating any conflict of interests.

After working in financial advisory industry over the past 20 years, the team at Integrity Wealth Solutions realized that the advisor’s bottom line should not come first; the client’s needs, interests and success should.

Thus, Integrity Wealth Solutions was born. We walked away from the traditional practice of charging a fee based on percentage of assets under management. We believe in transparency, and therefore moved into a straightforward and fair annual flat fee tiered compensation structure.

As an independent Registered Investment Advisor, registered with U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, Integrity Wealth Solutions is able to offer the independent, unbiased advice and solutions that our clients deserve.

Integrity Wealth Solutions is a privately owned, independent financial planning and wealth management firm. Integrity Wealth Solutions serves as a fiduciary and our business model is not predicated by the sale of proprietary financial products and we are not affiliated with any single company.

We attribute the success of our business to the cornerstones upon which we rely: integrity, process, competence and service. We believe that adherence to these four principles will result in long-term relationships with our clients, based on mutual trust and respect.

Company Description:
Davidson Capital Group LLC was founded in 1998 by Tom Davidson senior, and immediately raised $100 million on behalf of Ken Bajaj and AppNet Systems from Bruce Rauner at GTCR in Chicago to fund a roll-up transaction (soon to become a public company worth $1.2 billion).  DCG then raised $50 million for Ashok Thareja and Orblynx from Madison Dearborn, Spectrum Equity and Morgan Stanley Capital Partners. Especially over the next decade and to the present time, DCG widened its financing activities for companies in diverse industries, including financial services, energy, manufacturing, healthcare, medical devices, technology, telecommunications, food production, and others. While most DCG assignments have been in the USA, DCG also represents clients from a variety of foreign countries, most recently including Denmark, New Zealand and India.

DCG represents owners of companies in sell-side transactions and has produced very attractive sale results for their clients, for businesses as diverse as shipbuilding and food production and international sales.
   
A graduate of Williams College and Duke Law School, Tom began as a lawyer at Cravath, Swaine and Moore in New York City, representing many of the major national investment banks and corporations. He then became Senior Vice President and General Counsel for T. Boone Pickens at Mesa Petroleum in Texas, then in the same capacity for Robert E. Thomas at MAPCO, Inc., a broadly diversified $3 billion energy company headquartered in Tulsa, Oklahoma.  He was later Senior Vice President of a newly-created affiliate of Peter Kiewit Sons Inc., of Omaha, Nebraska, one of the nation's largest construction companies, related to their $2 billion acquisition of a national packaging company. 

Several times, Tom re-entered the law world to create Corporate and Securities Groups at major law firms in New York, and in Washington, D.C. for Akin, Gump, Strauss, Hauer and Feld. 

One of Tom's current interests is LeadershipForUS.

Title: Managing Director
Company Description: Westwood Capital, LLC is an investment bank and financial advisory firm founded in 1995.  It is a registered FINRA broker-dealer and provides a unique combination of expertise and experience in the firm's four general practice areas: Restructuring Advisory Services and Litigation Support, Project Development Finance and Real Estate Advisory Services, Securitization and Corporate Finance, and Mergers, Acquisitions and  Financial Advisory Services.  Industry leaders and innovators in their fields, Westwood's bankers have been engaged — for an average of nearly two decades each — in the creation and popularization of many forms of financing that are today widely accepted in the financial markets, but were originally custom-designed solutions for Westwood individual clients.  Westwood's principals have been an integral factor in the creation and execution of a number of signature transactions including the first commercial mortgage-backed securities offerings, the first issuance of investment-grade rated asset-backed securities as part of the reorganization of a bankrupt estate, the first investment-grade rated debt issue backed by gaming device revenues, the first major music royalty transaction, and the introduction of securities backed by Small Business Administration loans.   The M&A and financial advisory practice, together with the securitization and restructuring practices, have enabled Westwood to offer an extensive and complementary package of services to non-bank lenders and their shareholders. Over the years, Westwood has been a source of market knowledge for asset-based lenders, specialty lenders, leasing companies and factors, achieving unparalleled success in establishing a market for clients desiring to recognize value through business combinations or sales.  The firm is recognized for adding considerable value to situations involving complicated structures and companies with heavy dependence on repeated issuance of securitized debt.

Title: Founder
Company Product and Services: m&a , capital raise, founder advisory, strategic advisement, debt
Company Description:
Raising capital is harder than it has ever been. 

Only 2% of startups that apply to angel groups get funded. The average seed to Series A journey now takes more than two years. Those who get funded are a measly .05%. Most founders walk into that process underprepared and positioned to be quickly over-diluted.

I started HST Capital to change that.

HST Capital is a capital raise advisory practice founded by an entrepreneur, investment banker, and active venture capital fund manager. I bring all three perspectives to one question: is your business truly ready to attract investor capital, and are you leaving revenue on the table before you go after equity?

I start where most advisors don't. Before a pitch deck gets built or an investor list gets pulled, I evaluate your revenue engine. The most expensive capital you will ever raise is equity. The cheapest is a paying customer who validates your market, funds your growth, and tells every future investor that your business actually works.

From there, if it is determined a capital raise is needed, I help founders build compelling pitch materials, construct precision investor targeting lists, and develop the outreach system to run their own process with confidence. Founders who own their investor relationships close better terms.

No placement fees. No conflicts of interest. Just the strategy, frameworks, and materials to walk into every investor meeting as the most prepared person in
With us, you only go to market when you can execute a transaction at top of market. 

We seek out the least expensive capital available for our clients and protect their equity as if it was our own.

Company Description:
EASY ACCESS TO BUSINESS FRIENDLY FUNDING: 
Our firm serves as an intermediary to provide growing companies access to business friendly growth capital. We focus on emerging companies, with annual revenue between $3M and $50M, that are seeking $1M-$3.5M in growth capital. We specialize in Royalty Based Finance (RBF). 

ROYALTY BASED FINANCE: 
Royalty Based Finance is an innovative new source of growth capital that fits between debt financing and venture capital. Your company receives growth capital in exchange for a fixed percentage of the monthly top-line revenue. Payments are flexible, no personal guarantees or hard collateral required.

 UNLIKE TRADITIONAL FUNDING, BUSINESS OWNERS: 
•	Are Not Forced to Adhere to Restrictive Financial Covenants 
•	Do Not Provide Personal Guarantees or Warrants 
•	Retain Ownership and Control of the Company 
•	Make Flexible Monthly Payments

Title: Managing Partner
Company Industry: venture capital and private equity principals
Company Description:
Stoney River Capital Partners LLC (SRCP) is a business services firm that operates as a Facilitator or Consultant “bridge” between successful growing entrepreneurial organizations and the private equity investment communities.  We have developed deep communities on the company side as well as the investment side to bring these two groups together for a Win / Win situation.

Our Unique Value Proposition is that SRCP understands the needs and disconnects between the organizations it represents and the Private Equity and Banking Investment Communities.  It successfully “bridges” this disconnect by developing solid organizational infrastructures with our clients which allows investors to see the business aspects of the company, not just the products.

SRCP completes this bridge by putting our clients through an intensive pre-investment Due-Diligence Process to detail its current business environment.   We place this business knowledge in a signature Due Diligence Capital Investment Notebook.   Our Due Diligence Notebook is based on a composite collection of over 55 investor / company business structure “Templates” that form the core of the due diligence process.  These Templates plus a Company Position Paper, the Business Executive Summary, a One Page Overview and a structured focus PowerPoint presentation guideline form the core of what capital investors need to know.

This company business information and knowledge is completed prior to Investors evaluating the Company, minimizing the time needed for Investors to impose a “No Shop” clause in their term sheets, and answering most questions that will come up in the capital investment presentations   In-depth business information and knowledge is required by the private equity community for capital investment.

ALA feels too many ideas get stuck because many physicians or other inventors do not have experience raising or accessing capital and we hope to alleviate this leading to better patient outcomes. In addition to helping with capital raises, the firm advises companies, both large and small, on strategy, investor outreach, M&A, and IPOs. Figuring out innovative solutions to difficult problems is our daily passion. ALA focuses on quality over quantity, evaluating hundreds of companies but choosing to work with just a few each year. We expect our success rates to be much higher than average. The team largely works on a performance basis where much of any compensation is based upon results and we always align ourselves with the company's founders, management, and investors. When our clients do well, we do well. Title: Managing Director
Company Product and Services: venture investment, consulting, startup
Company Description:
Innovation: Introduction of new ideas
With the rapid changes in our environment due to the internet and globalization, we are seeing a great transformation of the “standard” which has overturned the foundation. By looking at things from a new viewpoint and doing something new or something different through our thoughts or actions will have its own meaning and value. ZVP will always search for, embrace and cultivate innovations and make contributions to create a new “standard”.

Investment: Allocate capital for future benefit
Evaluating the true value of a business is not only based on the size of the business or its stock value but also its innovation, future potential and most importantly, its people. ZVP’s role is to maximize resources including people’s knowledge, experience, time and energy to create opportunities which turns a risk into a benefit. The origin of the word “risk” in Italian “risicare” means “try with courage” and when our partner or client commits to a venture, ZVP will be a dedicated partner.

Independent: Think and act by one’s own will
tarting up a new business is similar to becoming a parent. Moreover, to operating a business is similar to raising a child. As the Chinese proverb says, “Give a man a fish and you feed him for a day. Teach a man to fish and you feed him for a lifetime”, ZVP helps our clients with not only their short term needs but we also guide them to independently empower their organization through sharing internal knowledge and wisdom.

Title: Director
Company Description:
Consortium Structured Investments (“Consortium”) is a boutique investment firm focusing on originating, structuring and managing the assets of tax credit-enhanced real estate developments across the country. Consortium’s established network of industry experts enables it to identify investment opportunities that can be tailored for particular investment criteria, including location, credit type, investment structure, yield parameters and risk profile. Consortium’s collaborative business philosophy allows it to join forces with real estate developers and investors to bring targeted real estate developments to fruition.

On behalf of various Fortune 500 financial, insurance and retail-oriented corporations, Consortium continues to structure and place equity investments utilizing the Federal Historic Rehabilitation Tax Credit program; the State Tax Credit programs of Delaware, Massachusetts, Missouri, North Carolina, Ohio, Rhode Island, South Carolina, Virginia and West Virginia; the Federal New Markets Tax Credit program; and the Federal Low Income Housing Tax Credit program. Collectively, Consortium has structured, closed and funded tax credit equity facilitating over $1 billion of real estate development.

Title: Managing Partner

Company Description:
Restaurant Management financing- Mergers & Acquisitions- Franchise Corporate finance - Sell-side & buy-side advisory services 

Capital Insight LLC ("Capital Insight"​ or "CI"​) is a nationally prominent middle-market/ upper middle-market investment bank focused on providing a full range of financial and operational advisory services to companies in industries with an investment concentration in multi-unit retail concepts such as restaurants and convenience and gasoline retail locations and other related assets. We also make principal investments in commercial real estate holdings and/or serve as capital market intermediaries to companies in various industries seeking to maximize the value of their fee-owned real estate holdings.

Quite simply, CI is a team of highly-experienced, senior-level professionals with complimentary skill sets. This allows us to bring a unique and collaborative approach to the services we provide. Whereas other firms typically offer a transaction service team comprised of one senior-level person supported by a team of mid-level and junior-level financial professionals, our senior-level collaborative approach is a distinct point of differentiation. Our professionals have successfully served as financial and operational experts, executive leaders and equity holders of companies in the industries we serve.

With an intricate knowledge about industry performance, best practices, enterprise and asset valuation trends and other major capital market issues supported by our expansive network of M&A and capital market participants, our team has completed sophisticated mergers and acquisitions; the origination of debt, sale-leaseback and equity capital; complex debt and corporate restructurings (both in and out of court); and interim operational and leadership engagements designed to achieve operational efficiency, improve profitability and maximize business enterprise value.
